mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in over 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and industrial production indicate synchronized demand and supply expansion, which compresses valuation multiples and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating inflation, rising consumer prices force central banks to tighten policy expectations, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or flat when CPIAUCSL accelerates above 0.3 percent month-over-month, or the signal fails to predict direction in high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or payroll growth fails to correlate with quarterly equity returns in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that precedes earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive weekly returns occur in over 60 percent of instances following three consecutive industrial production declines, or the signal shows no predictive edge in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with accelerating real GDP growth indicate strong residential and aggregate demand, which boosts financial sector profitability and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined housing and GDP signal yields negative forward returns in more than half of observed cases, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate models.

mechanism_narrative: A discrete federal funds rate hike exceeding 25 basis points increases the cost of capital and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25 percentage points in a single meeting
failure_condition: Forward quarterly returns are positive in over 65 percent of instances following a 25-basis-point rate hike, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample evaluation.
